Package org.eventb.core.ast.datatype

Interface IConstructorBuilder

All Known Implementing Classes:
ConstructorBuilder
public interface IConstructorBuilder
Common protocol for specifying a value constructor of a datatype. Instances of this interface are obtained by calling IDatatypeBuilder.addConstructor(String) with the name of the constructor to build.

Call addArgument(Type) or addArgument(String, Type) to specify the arguments of the constructor (and the corresponding destructors). For each argument, clients must specify its type. In this context, the datatype type parameters are represented as GivenTypes with the type parameter name and the datatype type is represented as a GivenType using the datatype name (without any reference to its type parameters).

E.g.: for the list datatype, List(S), reference to S must be done using the given type S and reference to List(S) using the given type List.

If a name is also specified for an argument, it denotes the corresponding destructor. Otherwise, the parameter is nameless and no destructor is generated.

If the argument type is available in string form, then clients can call DatatypeBuilder.parseType(String) to obtain a type following the above conventions.

Arguments can be added until this constructor is finalized (by finalizing the datatype builder that created it with IDatatypeBuilder.finalizeDatatype()).

Since:
3.0

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    void
    addArgument(String name, Type type)
    Adds a constructor argument together with the corresponding destructor.
    void
    addArgument(Type argType)
    Adds a constructor argument without any corresponding destructor.
    boolean
    isBasic()
    Tells whether this constructor is a basic constructor.

  • Method Details

    • addArgument

      void addArgument(String name, Type type)
      Adds a constructor argument together with the corresponding destructor.

      The type can be obtained by calling DatatypeBuilder.parseType(String) with a string representation of the argument type or must be constructed as follows:

      • A type definition using the datatype type must use a GivenType with the datatype name (instead of a parametric type)
      • A type definition must refer to a type parameter using a GivenType with the type parameter name
      • The datatype type must not occur within a powerset construct

      Parameters:
      name - the name of the corresponding destructor or null if there is no corresponding destructor
      type - the type of the constructor argument
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the given type was not created by the same factory has the datatype builder of this object
      IllegalArgumentException - if the datatype type occurs within a powerset in the argument type
      IllegalStateException - if this builder has been finalized
      See Also:
      • addArgument(Type)

    • addArgument

      void addArgument(Type argType)
      Adds a constructor argument without any corresponding destructor. This is a short-hand method fully equivalent to calling addArgument(null, argType).
      Parameters:
      argType - the type of the argument

    • isBasic

      boolean isBasic()
      Tells whether this constructor is a basic constructor. A basic constructor is such that none of its argument types reference the datatype type itself (no recursive use of the datatype).
      Returns:
      true iff this is a basic constructor
